question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call /network/options to trigger unbounded allocation of the operationStatuses slice via make([]*rTypes.OperationStatus, 0, len(types.GetTransactionResults())) on every request, exhausting heap memory and causing a non-network DoS crash of the Rosetta service?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a CPU-exhausting tight loop by sending concurrent /network/options requests that each iterate over the full types.GetTransactionResults() map (up to MaxTransactionResult=337 entries) and call persistence.IsTransactionResultSuccessful() per entry, causing sustained CPU saturation and non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by flooding /network/options such that the repeated construction of rTypes.NetworkOptionsResponse with the full errors.Errors slice and operationTypes slice on every call causes GC pressure and memory exhaustion without any rate limiting or caching?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending concurrent /network/status requests that each trigger two sequential database queries via RetrieveGenesis(ctx) and RetrieveLatest(ctx), exhausting the database connection pool and starving all other service operations?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by flooding /network/status such that addressBookEntryRepo.Entries(ctx) executes the latestNodeServiceEndpoints SQL query (with string_agg and multiple joins) on every call, exhausting database CPU and I/O resources?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that force addressBookEntryRepo.Entries(ctx) to iterate over both addressBook101 and addressBook102 file IDs when the first returns empty, doubling the number of database queries per request and amplifying DB load?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by triggering the peers.ToRosetta() call on an extremely large AddressBookEntries result set, causing unbounded memory allocation of []*rTypes.Peer and map[string]any metadata objects per peer, crashing the process?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that each call RetrieveGenesis(ctx) which internally calls initGenesisRecordFile(ctx) with a sync.Once, and if the genesis query is slow or blocked, all concurrent goroutines pile up waiting on the DB, exhausting goroutine stack memory?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that each allocate a new context with GetDbWithContext(ctx) inside Entries(), RetrieveGenesis(), and RetrieveLatest() without the service enforcing a global concurrency limit, leading to goroutine explosion and OOM?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkList()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by flooding /network/list with high-frequency requests, where each call allocates a new rTypes.NetworkListResponse wrapping the same n.network pointer, causing GC pressure from millions of short-lived allocations that degrade throughput for all other endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that NetworkOptions() has no caching and rebuilds the full operationStatuses slice from types.GetTransactionResults() on every invocation, making it a CPU-intensive hot path under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending concurrent /network/options requests that each call tools.GetStringValuesFromInt32StringMap(types.GetTransactionTypes()) and append types.OperationTypeFee, allocating a new []string slice per request without any result caching, leading to heap exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that trigger the full three-step sequential execution (RetrieveGenesis → RetrieveLatest → Entries) where each step holds a DB connection, and under high concurrency all DB connections are held simultaneously, causing a deadlock-like connection pool exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests with a context that has a very long deadline, causing GetDbWithContext(ctx) to hold DB connections open for extended periods, starving other legitimate requests of database resources?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the absence of a request-level timeout in NetworkStatus(), where a slow database response to the selectLatestWithIndex query causes goroutines to hang indefinitely, accumulating until memory is exhausted?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that each trigger addressBookEntryRepo.Entries() to execute the latestNodeServiceEndpoints query with string_agg aggregation over a large address_book_service_endpoint table, causing repeated full table scans that saturate DB I/O?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending concurrent /network/options requests that each iterate over the transactionResults map (shared global state initialized in init()) without a read lock, and if the map is ever mutated concurrently, trigger a fatal concurrent map read/write panic crashing the process?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that errors.Errors is a package-level slice included directly in every NetworkOptionsResponse, and if this slice is large, serializing it to JSON on every response causes excessive CPU usage under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that force the blockRepository to re-execute initGenesisRecordFile() when the genesis block is not yet cached (ConsensusStart == genesisConsensusStartUnset), causing a thundering herd of concurrent DB queries before sync.Once fires?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests during node startup when initGenesisRecordFile() returns ErrNodeIsStarting, causing the service to return errors but still consuming DB connection resources on every attempt, amplifying startup-phase resource exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the sequential nature of RetrieveGenesis(ctx) followed by RetrieveLatest(ctx) in NetworkStatus(), where each call independently acquires and releases a DB connection, creating a window for connection pool exhaustion under high concurrency?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that trigger peers.ToRosetta() to allocate a new map[string]any{} for each peer's Metadata field, and with a large address book, the cumulative allocation of thousands of maps per request causes GC pauses that degrade service availability?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending concurrent /network/options requests that each call persistence.IsTransactionResultSuccessful() for every entry in GetTransactionResults() (up to 337 entries), creating a CPU-bound hot path that starves other goroutines under GOMAXPROCS constraints?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NewNetworkAPIService()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by triggering repeated re-initialization of the networkAPIService (e.g., via a misconfigured dependency injection path) where each call to tools.GetStringValuesFromInt32StringMap(types.GetTransactionTypes()) allocates a new []string, causing memory pressure?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by sending requests that each call addressBookEntryRepo.Entries(ctx) which iterates over both file IDs (101 and 102) in a loop, and if both return empty results, both DB queries execute fully, doubling the per-request DB cost and amplifying resource exhaustion?
